lessons.title: Numbers 11-15
lessons.topic: Counting colorful balloons at a party
lessons.context: Counting colorful balloons at a party.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do we see lots of balloons?
questions.hint: Think about fun times with friends and cake!

questions.question_text: What colors can balloons be?
questions.hint: Look around! Balloons come in lots of pretty colors!

questions.question_text: How many is more than ten?

items.definition: 10 and 1 more
10 and 2 more
10 and 3 more
10 and 4 more
10 and 5 more

steps.title: Lily's Birthday Party
steps.text: It's Lily's birthday party! How many balloons are there? There are eleven red balloons and twelve blue balloons. There are thirteen yellow balloons. Mom brings fourteen green balloons and fifteen pink balloons. So many colorful balloons!
steps.scene_description: happy children at birthday party with many colorful balloons decorations

questions.question: How many red balloons are there?
What color are the twelve balloons?
Who brings the green balloons?

steps.rule_pattern: How many ___ are there? There are ___ ___ ___.
steps.explanation: We use 'How many' to ask about numbers, and we answer with 'There are' plus the number, color, and thing.
examples: How many balloons are there? There are eleven red balloons.
How many candles are there? There are twelve yellow candles.
How many cookies are there? There are fifteen brown cookies.

items.question: ___ are eleven blue flowers.
items.explanation: Use 'There are' to say how many

steps.audio_text: Hello, children! Look at Emma's birthday party. How many balloons are there? Let's count together! There are eleven red balloons and twelve yellow balloons. Oh, and there are fifteen blue balloons too! So many colorful balloons at the party!

questions.question: How many red balloons are there?
How many blue balloons are there?
What color are the twelve balloons?

steps.prompt: Practice counting balloons! Look at the picture and say the sentences.

prompts.prompt: Say: There are eleven red balloons.
Say: There are thirteen yellow balloons.
Ask your friend: How many blue balloons are there?
prompts.expected_text: How many blue balloons are there?

questions.question_text: How many balloons ___ there?
Which number comes after twelve?
Read: 'There are fifteen red balloons.' How many red balloons are there?
Choose the correct sentence:
True or False: We use 'How many' to ask about numbers.

lessons.title: Numbers 16-20
lessons.topic: Counting books on a library shelf
lessons.context: Counting books on a library shelf.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do we keep books at home or school?
questions.hint: Think about where books stand up in a row!

questions.question_text: How many fingers do you have on both hands?
questions.hint: Count the fingers on one hand, then the other hand!

questions.question_text: If you have 10 books and add more books, do you get a bigger or smaller number?
questions.hint: When we add more things, we count up!

items.sentence: I count ___ books on the top shelf.
There are ___ colorful crayons in my box.
I can count all the way to ___!

steps.title: Books at the Library
steps.text: Emma goes to the library. She counts books on the shelf. There are sixteen red books. There are seventeen blue books. There are eighteen green books. How many books are there?
steps.scene_description: young girl counting colorful books on library shelf

questions.question: Where does Emma go?
How many red books are there?
What does Emma do at the library?

steps.rule_pattern: How many ___ are there? There are ___ ___.
steps.explanation: We use 'How many' to ask about numbers, and we answer with 'There are' plus the number and thing we are counting.
examples: How many books are there? There are sixteen books.
How many crayons are there? There are nineteen crayons.
How many apples are there? There are twenty apples.

items.explanation: Use 'are' when asking about plural things

items.sentence: There are twenty book on the shelf.

items.question: How many books ___ there?
items.explanation: Use 'are' when asking about plural things

items.question: ___ many apples are there?
items.explanation: Use 'How many' to ask about numbers

steps.audio_text: Hello, children! Today we're going to count books at the library. Look at the shelf. How many red books are there? Let's count together: one, two, three... sixteen! There are sixteen red books. Now, how many blue books are there? Count with me: seventeen blue books! Great job, everyone!

questions.question: How many red books are there?
How many blue books are there?
Where are the children counting books?

steps.prompt: Practice speaking! Say these sentences out loud.

prompts.prompt: Say: How many books are there?
Say: There are twenty red books on the shelf.
prompts.expected_text: There are twenty red books on the shelf.

questions.question_text: How do you say the number 17?
There ___ twenty books on the shelf.
How many ___ are there?
True or False: We say 'There are sixteen book.'
Which number comes after nineteen?

lessons.title: New Colors
lessons.topic: Describing the colors of a classroom rainbow
lessons.context: Describing the colors of a classroom rainbow.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What colors do you see in a rainbow?
questions.hint: Think about the bright colors you see in the sky after rain!

questions.question_text: Where can we see rainbows?
questions.correct_answer: in the sky and in our classroom
options: in the sky and in our classroom
questions.hint: Rainbows can be outside AND inside when we make them!

questions.question_text: What color comes after red in a rainbow?
questions.hint: It's a color that looks like a yummy fruit!

steps.title: Our Classroom Rainbow
steps.text: Look at our classroom rainbow! There are purple and grey stripes. There are brown and white stripes too. The black stripe is at the bottom. How many colors are there? There are six colors in our rainbow!
steps.scene_description: children looking at a colorful rainbow display on classroom wall

questions.question: Where is the black stripe?
How many colors are in the classroom rainbow?
What colors does the story mention together?

steps.rule_pattern: How many ___ are there? There are [number] [color] ___.
steps.explanation: We use this pattern to ask about numbers and tell how many colored things we see.
examples: How many crayons are there? There are five purple crayons.
How many cats are there? There are two black cats.
How many chairs are there? There are six brown chairs.

items.correctSentence: How many grey clouds are there?

items.explanation: Use 'are' when asking about many things

items.question: How many ___ there?
items.explanation: Use 'are' when asking about many things

items.question: How ___ pencils are there?
items.explanation: Use 'many' to ask about countable things

steps.audio_text: Good morning, class! Look at our beautiful rainbow on the wall. How many colors are there? Let's count together. There are purple, grey, brown, white, black, and one more. Yes! There are six colors in our classroom rainbow. The purple stripe is at the top and the black stripe is at the bottom.

questions.question: How many colors are in the classroom rainbow?
What color is at the top of the rainbow?
Where is the black stripe?

steps.prompt: Let's practice talking about colors! Listen and repeat these sentences.
steps.expected_text: There are six colors in the rainbow.

prompts.prompt: Look at the rainbow. Say: There are six colors.
Now ask me: How many purple crayons are there?
prompts.expected_text: How many purple crayons are there?

prompts.prompt: Tell me: There are three brown chairs in the classroom.
prompts.expected_text: There are three brown chairs in the classroom.

questions.question_text: What color is this? 🟣
There ___ five brown chairs.
How many colors are in a rainbow?
True or False: We say 'How many are there?' to ask about numbers.
Which sentence is correct?

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.topic: Counting colored objects and identifying higher numbers
lessons.context: Counting colored objects and identifying higher numbers.

steps.instruction: Let's think about numbers and colors! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: How many red apples do you see?
questions.hint: Count carefully! Point to each apple as you count: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5!

questions.question_text: Which group has MORE toys?
questions.hint: Count each group. Which number is the biggest? 8 is more than 6, 4, and 3!

questions.question_text: What color are these 10 balloons?
questions.hint: Look at all the balloons. They are all the same color. What color is it?

items.definition: The number that comes after ten
The number between fourteen and sixteen
Two groups of ten
The color you make when you mix red and blue
The color of chocolate and tree trunks
The color of snow and clouds
Many colors in the sky after rain
The color between black and white
The number after eleven
The number that comes after twelve
The number between fifteen and seventeen
The number that comes after seventeen
The color of night and dark things
The color of grapes and eggplants
The color of bears and cookies
Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple in the sky

steps.title: Emma's Toy Shop
steps.text: Emma has a toy shop. How many toys are there? There are fifteen purple balls and twelve brown teddy bears. There are eighteen grey elephants and twenty white rabbits. Emma loves her colorful toy shop!
steps.scene_description: young girl in a colorful toy shop with stuffed animals and balls on shelves

questions.question: How many brown teddy bears are there?
What color are the rabbits?
Does Emma have twenty white rabbits?

steps.rule_pattern: How many ___ are there? There are ___ ___ ___.
steps.explanation: We use 'How many' to ask about numbers, and 'There are' to tell how many colored things we can see.
examples: How many red apples are there? There are fifteen red apples.
How many blue cars are there? There are twelve blue cars.
How many yellow stars are there? There are twenty yellow stars.

steps.rule_pattern: How many are there? / There are [Number] [Color] [Objects].

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Let's count together. Look at my basket. How many oranges are there? There are seventeen orange oranges! Now look at the box. How many pencils are there? There are thirteen grey pencils. Great counting, everyone!

questions.question: How many oranges are there?
What color are the pencils?
Where are the oranges?

steps.prompt: Look at the pictures and practice speaking! Count the colored objects and tell me about them.

prompts.prompt: Look at the red apples. Count them and say: There are ___ red apples.
Now look at the purple balloons. Ask me: How many purple balloons are there?
prompts.expected_text: How many purple balloons are there?

prompts.prompt: Count the brown cookies and tell me: There are ___ brown cookies.

questions.question_text: How many ___ are there? There are fifteen red apples.
What number comes after nineteen?
Which color is this? 🟣
True or False: We say 'There are twelve brown bears' to tell how many brown bears we see.
Count the stars: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ How many stars are there?
